Title: Sarpagan bridge enzyme has substrate-controlled cyclization and aromatization modes

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway entry product and directly creates scaffold for sarpagan/ajmalan alkaloid branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal monoterpene indole alkaloids include anti-arrhythmic compounds such as ajmaline
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts strictosidine-derived geissoschizine to polyneuridine aldehyde through oxidative cyclization (sarpagan bridge formation); can also aromatize tetrahydro-β-carboline substrates to β-carbolines depending on substrate structure
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with known MIA biosynthetic genes in Rauwolfia serpentina organs based on transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by transcriptome coexpression and functionally validated in heterologous systems; also localized to endoplasmic reticulum using YFP fusion in Catharanthus roseus cells; a homolog in Rauvolfia tetraphylla was silenced to reduce ajmaline accumulation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsSBE (CYP71AY4) was identified as the missing sarpagan bridge enzyme from Rauwolfia serpentina. 2. It converts geissoschizine into polyneuridine aldehyde, opening entry into sarpagan/ajmalan alkaloid biosynthesis. 3. The enzyme also catalyzes aromatization of tetrahydro-β-carbolines such as tetrahydroalstonine to alstonine, demonstrating substrate-controlled reaction mode.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid pathway; sarpagan/ajmalan branch and β-carboline-forming route
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast microsome enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; kinetic assay; subcellular localization; virus-induced gene silencing of homolog in Rauvolfia tetraphylla
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful gateway enzyme for engineering production of sarpagan/ajmalan and related β-carboline alkaloids in heterologous hosts; should be combined with upstream strictosidine-to-geissoschizine steps and downstream ajmalan enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of polyneuridine aldehyde and enables vinorine/ajmalan pathway reconstruction; also produces alstonine from tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots are the main early biosynthetic site in Rauwolfia serpentina; ajmaline reduction upon silencing was detected from Rauvolfia tetraphylla leaves due to systemic silencing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Ajmaline-related monoterpene indole alkaloids; polyneuridine aldehyde; alstonine
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast microsome assays with NADPH; VIGS in Rauvolfia tetraphylla
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics/coexpression analysis using publicly available Rauwolfia serpentina transcriptome data (medicinalplantgenomics.msu.edu)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR) in yeast expression system; functionally coupled in pathway reconstruction with SGD; GS; PNAE; VS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-entry cyclization enzyme for sarpagan/ajmalan scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTRBO transient expression vector in Nicotiana benthamiana (promoter type not stated)

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway entry product and directly creates scaffold for sarpagan-type/related alkaloid branch; also supports β-carboline aromatization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geissoschizine to polyneuridine aldehyde by oxidative cyclization and can aromatize tetrahydro-β-carboline substrates to β-carbolines depending on substrate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected by RT-PCR in Gelsemium sempervirens tissues including roots; exact strongest tissue not clearly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologous SBE from Gelsemium sempervirens supporting conserved chemistry across MIA-producing plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsSBE (CYP71AY5) is a homologous P450 from Gelsemium sempervirens with SBE activity. 2. It catalyzes both cyclization and aromatization reactions depending on substrate. 3. This supports a shared enzymatic mechanism across distinct alkaloid-producing species.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid pathway; sarpagan-type and β-carboline-forming chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; RT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate biocatalyst for heterologous production of Gelsemium-related and sarpagan-type alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes oxidative cyclization/aromatization leading to polyneuridine aldehyde or alstonine-class products from different substrates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Polyneuridine aldehyde; alstonine; sarpagan/β-carboline-related monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR) in yeast expression system

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms β-carboline alkaloid product from tetrahydro-β-carboline precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Alstonine is a monoterpene indole alkaloid with reported bioactivity; study emphasizes alkaloid scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Aromatizes tetrahydroalstonine to alstonine; compared with SBE to define substrate-controlled cyclization versus aromatization specificity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Analyzed using publicly available Catharanthus roseus expression data
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as comparative homolog and subjected to site-directed mutagenesis to probe residues underlying specificity relative to RsSBE
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrAS (CYP71AY1) is a homologous P450 from Catharanthus roseus that functions as an alstonine synthase. 2. It preferentially catalyzes aromatization rather than cyclization. 3. Mutagenesis comparing CrAS and RsSBE was used to probe determinants of reaction specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid pathway; β-carboline/alstonine branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome enzyme assay; site-directed mutagenesis; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for engineering alstonine and related β-carboline alkaloid production; comparative template for tuning P450 product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes alstonine formation by aromatization of tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Publicly available Catharanthus roseus transcriptome data (medicinalplantgenomics.msu.edu)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR) in yeast expression system
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch step leading to β-carboline alkaloid
